project_overview.label: project_overview
project_overview.summary: Traced request flow: endpoints.ts → rate_limit_middleware → auth_middleware → service handlers. Main components: auth (token, queue, session), API (endpoints, users), config, middleware.
keyFacts: Middleware chain: rate limit → auth → route handler
Four subsystems: auth, api, config, middleware

security_review.label: security_review
security_review.summary: Critical finding: tokens are unsigned base64 JSON — anyone can forge valid tokens. Rate limiting bypassable via X-Forwarded-For spoofing. No input validation on registration.
keyFacts: Tokens are base64 JSON with no signature — trivially forgeable
Rate limiting trusts X-Forwarded-For header — spoofable
Fix path: JWT signing for tokens, trusted proxy config for rate limiting

test_coverage_assessment.label: test_coverage_assessment
test_coverage_assessment.summary: Auth has decent test coverage (token, queue, rate limiter). API has basic endpoint tests. Integration tests are shallow. Rate limiting had zero tests before recent work. No tests for config validation or middleware.
test_coverage_assessment.placeholder: Test coverage: auth OK, rest shallow
keyFacts: tests/api/: 1 file, basic happy path only
tests/integration/: 1 file, doesn't test refresh or rate limiting
No tests for: config validation, middleware, session store

historical_decisions.label: historical_decisions
historical_decisions.summary: Used session_lookup to review auth queue and token expiry decisions. Confirmed: queue migration was necessary (mutex deadlocks), clock skew fix added 5s grace, both well-tested. Compressed earlier topics with compress_blob.
keyFacts: Auth queue: mutex→queue due to deadlocks, well-documented
Clock skew: 5s grace period, comprehensive tests added
Used compress_blob to reduce project_overview topic

msg_s5_1.summary: User request: Project overview, trace request handling flow

msg_s5_2.summary: Read README and directory structure. Traced handleRequest: rate limiting check → public route check → token validation → route handling. Components: src/auth (4 files), src/api (2 files), src/config (1 file), src/middleware (2 files).

msg_s5_3.summary: User request: Security review of auth middleware and rate limiting

msg_s5_4.summary: Critical: token_manager.ts encodes tokens as plain base64 JSON. No HMAC, no JWT signature. Any client can forge: Buffer.from(JSON.stringify(fakePayload)).toString('base64'). Rate limiting uses req.headers['x-forwarded-for'] which is client-controlled.

msg_s5_5.summary: User request: How severe is unsigned tokens? What's the fix path?

msg_s5_6.summary: Severity: critical in production, acceptable in dev/demo. Fix: replace base64 encode/decode with JWT sign/verify using jose or jsonwebtoken library. Rate limiting fix: configure trusted proxy list, fall back to socket remote address.

msg_s5_7.summary: User request: Assess test coverage across the codebase

msg_s5_8.summary: Surveyed tests/: auth/ has 3 files (token, queue, rate_limiter) with reasonable coverage. api/ has 1 file (endpoints) with happy path only. integration/ has 1 file that skips refresh. No middleware or config tests.

msg_s5_9.summary: User request: Use session_lookup for 'auth queue' and 'token expiry' decisions

msg_s5_10.summary: Found 2 relevant sessions via session_lookup. Auth queue investigation confirmed mutex deadlocks and documented rollback flag. Token expiry bugfix session traced the wrong turn (queue suspicion) and landed on clock skew fix.

msg_s5_11.summary: User request: Call context_map, compress done topics with compress_blob

msg_s5_12.summary: Context map shows 4 topics. Compressed project_overview to placeholder (already captured in summary). Security review and test coverage kept at full — still actively relevant.

msg_s5_13.summary: User request: Top 5 tech debt items by risk, and ONE thing to fix before production

msg_s5_14.summary: Top 5: (1) unsigned tokens — critical, (2) session store leak — moderate, (3) no config validation — moderate, (4) rate limit header spoofing — moderate, (5) shallow integration tests. #1 to fix before production: token signing. Without it, any client can impersonate any user.
